---
title: "Advanced Bioinformatics 2026 assessment"
author: "m2203099"
date: "2026-04-23"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(shiny)
```

**Task 1. Sum of all integers between 5 and 55**

```{r}
sum(5:55)
```
**Task 2. Sum of all integers between 5 and n**

```{r}
sumfun <- function(n)
  return(sum(5:n))

sumfun(10)
sumfun(20)
sumfun(100)
```
**Task 3. The Fibonnaci series**

```{r}
# Make a vector to hold 12 numbers
fib <- numeric(12)

# Define the first two values of the sequence
fib[1] <- 1
fib[2] <- 1

# Use a for loop to calculate the rest (starting from index 3)
for (i in 3:12) {
  fib[i] <- fib[i-1] + fib[i-2]
}

print(fib)
```
**Task 4. mtcars ggplot**

```{r}
ggplot(data=mtcars, aes(x=as.factor(gear), y=mpg, fill = as.factor(gear))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x="Number of Gears", y="Miles Per Gallon", fill="Gears")
```

**Task 5. A linear relationship between speed and breaking distance**

```{r}
Model <- lm(dist ~ speed, data=cars)

summary(Model)
?cars
```
**Task 6. ggplot of Task 5 data**

```{r}
ggplot(cars, aes(x=speed, y=dist)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method="lm")
```

**Task 7. lm and ggplot - more complex**
```{r}
# 1. Convert units to be useful (mph to feet per second)
cars$speed_fps <- cars$speed * 1.467

# 2. Create the transformed Y variable
cars$y_ratio <- cars$dist / cars$speed_fps

# 3. Fit the linear model
Model2 <- lm(y_ratio ~ speed_fps, data=cars)

# 4. View the results
summary(Model2)
```
```{r}
# 5. Plot it to show the straight line
ggplot(cars, aes(x=speed_fps, y=y_ratio)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(y = "Distance / Speed (Reaction Time + k*speed)",
       x = "Speed (ft/s)")
```

**Task 8. Load the data onto Rstudio**
```{r,collapse=T,warning=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
counts <- read.csv("exercise1_counts.csv", header = T, row.names = 1)

sample_info <- read.table("exercise1_sample_description.info", sep = "\t", header = TRUE)
```

**Task 9. Create col_data and check dimensions**
```{r,collapse=T,warning=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
# Create col data from sample description
col_data <- data.frame(filename = sample_info$filename, sample = sample_info$sample, condition = sample_info$condition, batch = sample_info$batch)
head(col_data)

# Clean the filename
col_data$filename <- gsub("TMC_", "", col_data$filename)

# Check the dimensions
all(sort(colnames(counts)) == sort(col_data$filename))
```

**Task 10. Construct DESeqDataSet object using count data and sample description. **
```{r,collapse=T,warning=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
# Load DESeq2 library
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(DESeq2))

# Create DESeq object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts, colData = col_data, design =~ condition)
dds

# Normalize using DESeq
dds <- DESeq(dds)
```

**Task 11. Perform rlog and VST transformation on the data. **
```{r,collapse=T,warning=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
# Normalize using rlog2
rlog2 <- rlog(dds)
rlog2_counts <- assay(rlog2)
# Normalize using vst
vst <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds)
vst_counts <- assay(vst)
```

**Task 12. Draw a heatmap of count matrix based on the top 40 highly expressed genes using rlog and VST data. **
```{r,collapse=T,warning=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
# Install heatmap package
# install.packages("pheatmap")
library(pheatmap)
# Select top 40 highly expressed genes
select <- order(rowMeans(counts(dds, normalized = TRUE)), decreasing = TRUE)[1:40]
# Heatmap using rlog data
pheatmap(assay(rlog2)[select, ])
# Heatmap using VST data
pheatmap(assay(vst)[select, ])
```

**Task 13. Generate a SDM to see the clustering of count data. **
```{r,collapse=T,warning=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
# Compute sample distance matrix
sdm <- as.matrix(dist(t(rlog2_counts)))
```

**Task 14. Perform the Principal Component Analysis using rlog method and find out the % significance values of first two principal components. **
```{r,collapse=T,warning=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
rlog2_counts <- rlog2_counts[rowSums(rlog2_counts) > 10, ]
pca <- prcomp(t(rlog2_counts), scale = TRUE)
summary(pca)
```

**Task 15. Repeat the PCA, this time using VST method and compare the plots with the ones obtained using rlog method. **
```{r,collapse=T,warning=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
pca <- prcomp(t(vst_counts), scale = TRUE)
summary(pca)
```
